message.content: ["Mustatil expands as a GIS-level AI vision workspace with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, and multi-model remote-sensing workflows.", "The release focuses on stability and practical usability.", "The existing AI Pipeline tab remains unchanged, while the surrounding detection and GIS tooling has been extended.", "New and improved components include additional support for Faster R-CNN, Mask R-CNN, U-Net and SAM/SAM2-based workflows, optional video detection."]
message.reasoning_content:
We need to output a JSON array of 2-4 short strings, each a self-contained facet from the text. The text describes an update to Mustatil, an open-source AI and GIS framework. Break it into facets: separate aspects, conditions, sub-claims. The text mentions: expansion of framework as GIS-level AI vision workspace, improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, multi-model remote-sensing workflows. Focus: stability and practical usability; existing AI Pipeline tab unchanged, surrounding detection and GIS tooling extended. New components: additional support for Faster R-CNN, Mask R-CNN, U-Net, SAM/SAM2-based workflows, optional video detection. So facets could be:

1. Expansion of Mustatil as a GIS-level AI vision workspace (with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, multi-model remote-sensing workflows).
2. Stability and practical usability focus.
3. Unchanged AI Pipeline tab and extended detection/GIS tooling.
4. New/improved components: support for specific models and optional video detection.

- "This update expands Mustatil as a GIS-level AI vision workspace with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, and multi-model remote-sensing workflows." -> facet: "expands Mustatil as a GIS-level AI vision workspace with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, and multi-model remote-sensing workflows" (could be split? But it's one aspect: expansion. I'll keep as one self-contained string, but maybe split into "improved support for geospatial processing", etc. However instruction: "distinct FACETS - the separate aspects, conditions or sub-claims it actually contains" so I can pull out discrete claims. I'll produce:

- "Mustatil expands as a GIS-level AI vision workspace with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, and multi-model remote-sensing workflows."
- "The release focuses on stability and practical usability."
- "The existing AI Pipeline tab remains unchanged, while the surrounding detection and GIS tooling has been extended."
- "New and improved components include additional support for Faster R-CNN, Mask R-CNN, U-Net and SAM/SAM2-based workflows, optional video detection."

But that's 4 facets. They are short, self-contained, no overlap. I'll check: facet 1 includes the expansion and improvements, but it's not overlapping with the others. The others are separate claims. So output as JSON array:

["Mustatil expands as a GIS-level AI vision workspace with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, and multi-model remote-sensing workflows.", "The release focuses on stability and practical usability.", "The existing AI Pipeline tab remains unchanged, while the surrounding detection and GIS tooling has been extended.", "New and improved components include additional support for Faster R-CNN, Mask R-CNN, U-Net and SAM/SAM2-based workflows, optional video detection."]

Check text: "This update expands Mustatil as a GIS-level AI vision workspace with improved support for geospatial processing, model experimentation, and multi-model remote-sensing workflows." Yes. So facet 1 is accurate. "The release focuses on stability and practical usability:" from text. The text says "The release focuses on stability and practical usability: the existing AI Pipeline tab remains unchanged, while the surrounding detection and GIS tooling has been extended." So I can treat the colon as connecting, but the clause after colon is a separate facet? I could combine "The release focuses on stability and practical usability: ..." but the instruction to split facets might separate that. I'll keep two separate facets: one about focus, one about unchanged tab and extended tooling. That yields 4. I'll output exactly that. No need to invent more.
